title: The Expansion of a Western Territory
article_text: I read an essay about a western territory where the economy had grown naturally over time. The local currency was initially based on gold, but later a digital database was introduced to track all transactions. Whenever a new stream of trade appeared, the territory's expansion happened constantly, and its boundaries shifted. The height of the mountains formed a natural boundary that was rarely crossed by people. It was apparent that the ratio of farmland to forest had changed badly after the expansion. Many people were scared that the changes would scare away the wildlife that fascinates visitors. The muscle of the local workers was vital for building new roads and houses. They had to spare some land for everyday needs like parks and schools. If the territory had not expanded so fast, the people would not have screamed in protest. The scream of the crowd was heard from a great distance, and the government promised to listen.

sentences.en: I read an essay about a western territory where the economy had grown naturally over time.
sentences.zh: 我读了一篇关于一个西部领土的文章，那里的经济随着时间的推移自然增长。

words.eng_def: a short piece of writing on a particular subject
an area of land controlled by a particular country or ruler
in a way that happens without being made to happen

sentences.en: The local currency was initially based on gold, but later a digital database was introduced to track all transactions.
sentences.zh: 当地的货币最初以黄金为基础，但后来引入了数字数据库来跟踪所有交易。

words.eng_def: the money used in a particular country
a large amount of information stored in a computer

sentences.en: Whenever a new stream of trade appeared, the territory's expansion happened constantly, and its boundaries shifted.
sentences.zh: 每当出现新的贸易流时，领土的扩张就不断发生，其边界也随之改变。

words.eng_def: a small river; a continuous flow of something
the act of becoming larger or more extensive
a line that marks the limits of an area

sentences.en: The height of the mountains formed a natural boundary that was rarely crossed by people.
sentences.zh: 山脉的高度形成了一道天然边界，很少有人跨越。

words.eng_def: the measurement of how tall something is

sentences.en: It was apparent that the ratio of farmland to forest had changed badly after the expansion.
sentences.zh: 很明显，扩张之后农田与森林的比例变得很糟糕。

words.eng_def: the relationship between two numbers showing how much bigger one is than the other
in a way that is not good; very much

sentences.en: Many people were scared that the changes would scare away the wildlife that fascinates visitors.
sentences.zh: 许多人担心这些变化会吓跑那些令游客着迷的野生动物。

sentences.en: The muscle of the local workers was vital for building new roads and houses.
sentences.zh: 当地工人的体力对于修建新的道路和房屋至关重要。

words.eng_def: the tissue in the body that can contract to produce movement; physical strength

words.def: 至关重要的，必不可少的

sentences.en: They had to spare some land for everyday needs like parks and schools.
sentences.zh: 他们不得不匀出一些土地用于公园和学校等日常需求。

words.eng_def: to give or use something that is extra; extra and not used
usual; happening or used every day

sentences.en: If the territory had not expanded so fast, the people would not have screamed in protest.
sentences.zh: 如果领土没有扩张得这么快，人们就不会尖叫抗议。

words.eng_def: to make a loud, high sound because of fear, anger, or excitement

sentences.en: The scream of the crowd was heard from a great distance, and the government promised to listen.
sentences.zh: 人群的尖叫声从很远的地方就能听到，政府承诺倾听。

words.eng_def: a loud, high sound made by someone who is frightened, angry, or excited

vocabulary.eng_def: in a way that happens without being made to happen

vocabulary.eng_def: the money used in a particular country
vocabulary.example: The euro is the currency of many European countries.

vocabulary.example: The company has a database of all its customers.

vocabulary.eng_def: at the beginning
vocabulary.example: Initially, I did not like the idea, but later I changed my mind.

vocabulary.eng_def: an area of land controlled by a particular country or ruler
vocabulary.example: The army defended its territory from attack.

vocabulary.eng_def: a small river; a continuous flow of something
vocabulary.example: A stream of water ran through the valley.

vocabulary.eng_def: not often
vocabulary.example: She rarely eats meat.

vocabulary.eng_def: the measurement of how tall something is
vocabulary.example: The height of the building is 50 meters.

vocabulary.eng_def: easy to see or understand
vocabulary.example: It was apparent that he was tired.

vocabulary.eng_def: in or from the west
vocabulary.example: California is a western state.

vocabulary.eng_def: the act of becoming larger or more extensive
vocabulary.example: The expansion of the city brought new jobs.

vocabulary.eng_def: all the time; without stopping
vocabulary.example: The baby cried constantly.

vocabulary.eng_def: the tissue in the body that can contract to produce movement; physical strength
vocabulary.example: He exercised to build his muscles.

vocabulary.eng_def: to make someone feel frightened
vocabulary.example: The loud noise scared the cat.

vocabulary.eng_def: in a way that is not good; very much
vocabulary.example: He was badly hurt in the accident.

vocabulary.eng_def: usual; happening or used every day
vocabulary.example: These are my everyday shoes.

vocabulary.eng_def: a line that marks the limits of an area
vocabulary.example: The river forms the boundary between the two countries.

vocabulary.eng_def: the relationship between two numbers showing how much bigger one is than the other
vocabulary.example: The ratio of boys to girls in the class is 2:1.

vocabulary.eng_def: a short piece of writing on a particular subject
vocabulary.example: She wrote an essay about her summer vacation.

vocabulary.eng_def: to make a loud, high sound because of fear, anger, or excitement
vocabulary.example: The children screamed with joy.
